revision.comment: Text replacement - "市の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Suitengu |image = {{ill|File:Otaru Suitengu Torii.jpg|ja|小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg|lt=270px|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Otaru Suitengu Torii.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Torii and Approach {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|11|34|N|141|0|15|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{coord|43|11|34|N|141|0|15|E|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|qq=}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|qq=}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|qq=}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|qq=}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|qq=}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:小樽の Municipal History]] [[Category:小樽市指定歴史的建造物]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:qq]]

neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Suitengu |image = {{ill|File:Otaru Suitengu Torii.jpg|ja|小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg|lt=270px|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Otaru Suitengu Torii.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Torii and Approach {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|11|34|N|141|0|15|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{coord|43|11|34|N|141|0|15|E|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|qq=}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|qq=}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|qq=}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|qq=}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|qq=}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} Otaru]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24316227]] Wikidata name: Suitengū shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suitengu |image = {{ill|File:Otaru Suitengu Torii.jpg|ja|小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg|lt=270px|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Otaru Suitengu Torii.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Torii and Approach {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|11|34|N|141|0|15|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{coord|43|11|34|N|141|0|15|E|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|qq=}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|qq=}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|qq=}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|qq=}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|qq=}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|qq=}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|qq=}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|qq=}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|qq=}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Designated Historic Buildings in Otaru]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]]

revision.comment: Bot: add wikidata link templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24316227]] Wikidata name: Suitengū shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suitengu |image = File:小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|43.19277777777778|N|141.00416666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Otaru|qid=Q242108}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|qq=|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|qq=|1=Sakaimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|qq=|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|qq=|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|qq=|1=Otaru Journal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]][[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Designated Historic Buildings in Otaru]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] [[sn:Suitengū (Otaru City)]] {{wikidata link|Q24316227}}

Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Designated Historic Buildings in Otaru]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q24316227}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Otaru Suitengu]][[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24316227]] Wikidata name: Suitengū shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suitengu |image = File:小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|43.19277777777778|N|141.00416666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Otaru|qid=Q242108}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|qq=|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|qq=|1=Sakaimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|qq=|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|qq=|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|qq=|1=Otaru Journal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Otaru|WD=Q242108}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|qq=|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|qq=|1=Sakaimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|qq=|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|qq=|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|qq=|1=Otaru Journal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|qq=|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Designated Historic Buildings in Otaru]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q24316227}}

neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Designated Historic Buildings in Otaru]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q24316227}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Otaru Suitengu]][[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24316227]] Wikidata name: Suitengū shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suitengu |image = File:小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|43.19277777777778|N|141.00416666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Otaru|WD=Q242108}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|1=Sakaimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|1=Otaru Journal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|1=Otaru Journal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Designated Historic Buildings in Otaru]] [[Category:Suiten-gū_Shrines]] {{wikidata link|Q24316227}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Otaru Suitengu]][[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24316227]] Wikidata name: Suitengū shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suitengu |image = File:小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|43.19277777777778|N|141.00416666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]

Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|1=Sakaimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Otaru|WD=Q242108}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|1=Sakaimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|1=Otaru Journal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Otaru Suitengu]][[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24316227]] Wikidata name: Suitengū shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suitengu |image = File:小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg |location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|43.19277777777778|N|141.00416666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]]
[[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = [[:en:1859|1859]] |main_hall = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = [[:en:June 15|June 15]] |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Otaru|WD=Q242108}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] [[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]]. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for [[:en:Cherry blossom|Cherry Blossoms]] in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|1=Sakaimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=[[:en:Otaru|Otaru City]] |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the [[:en:Takuboku Ishikawa|Ishikawa Takuboku]] poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|1=Otaru Journal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Yatsunahome deity]] (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]] (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * [[:en:1859|1859]] ([[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 6), established. * [[:en:1900|1900]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] was enshrined alongside. * [[:en:1919|1919]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q24316227}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT
