revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Otaru Suitengu]][[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Otaru Suitengu]][[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suitengu |image = File:小樽水天宮の鳥居.jpg |location = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}{{ill|Otaru|en|Otaru|WD=Q242108|lt=Otaru City|1=Otaru}} Aioi-cho 3-1 |coordinates = {{Coord|43.19277777777778|N|141.00416666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Yatsunahome deity|1=Mizuhanome}}
{{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |sacred_object = |shrine_status = Former {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = {{ill|1859|en|1859|WD=Q7687|lt=1859|1=1859}} |main_hall = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |alias = |ticket_places = |annual_festival = {{ill|June 15|en|June 15|WD=Q2613|lt=June 15|1=June 15}} |rituals = |map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=11|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=141|lon_min=0|lon_sec=15|label=Suitengu|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Otaru|WD=Q242108|1=Otaru}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} }} '''Suitengu''' (水天宮) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} {{ill|Otaru|en|Otaru|WD=Q242108|lt=Otaru City|1=Otaru}}. Its former status was that of a {{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is sited at a location offering a panoramic view of {{ill|Otaru Port|ja|小樽港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Port|1=Port of Otaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10959354}}, and is renowned as one of the famous spots for {{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=Cherry Blossoms|1=Cherry blossom}} in Otaru City. The steep stairs and slopes descending toward the seaside area of {{ill|Sakaimachi|ja|堺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaimachi|1=Q11428313|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428313}} are known as Gaijin-zaka{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1402.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 前編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher={{ill|Otaru|en|Otaru|WD=Q242108|lt=Otaru City|1=Otaru}} |accessdate=2016-05-24}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.otaru.lg.jp/simin/koho/sakamati/1403.html |title=コッフさんと外人坂 後編 |work=おたる坂まち散歩 |publisher=小樽市 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. A straight road connects the shrine to {{ill|Otaru Park|ja|小樽公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Park|1=Otaru Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24316228}}, located on the hillside, thereby integrating the surrounding landscape{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.jila-hokkaido.com/gyouji_/kitanozouensisan/kitanozoen_all/4_otaru/Isan-4(Otaru).pdf |title=小樽公園 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja|日本造園学会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Landscape Architecture Society|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造園|1=Landscape architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47844}} Hokkaido Branch |accessdate=2016-05-12}}. Within the precincts, there stand monuments such as the {{ill|Takuboku Ishikawa|en|Takuboku Ishikawa|WD=Q467747|lt=Ishikawa Takuboku|1=Takuboku Ishikawa}} poetry monument, the Mitsuya Yōson (みつやようそん) haiku monument, and the {{ill|Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|ja|河邨文一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasomura Bun'ichirō|1=Bun'ichirō Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11554131}} poetry monument{{Cite web|和書|url=http://otaru-journal.com/2005/10/post-837.php |title=水天宮の啄木歌碑を移設！新たな装いでお目見え！ |date=2005-10-15 |work={{ill|Otaru Journal|ja|小樽ジャーナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaru Journal|1=Q11462138|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11462138}} |publisher=小樽ジャーナル社 |accessdate=2016-05-24}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrines sharing the same name as the Suitengu of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume City|1=Kurume}}, designated as the head shrine, enshrine figures including {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}, whereas the deities enshrined here are different and do not belong to that lineage{{Sfn|小野|1999|p=103}}. * {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Yatsunahome deity|1=Mizuhanome}} (Mizuhanome-no-Kami) * {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} (Ukemochi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Kami) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|lt_ja=伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Kami) == History == * {{ill|1859|en|1859|WD=Q7687|lt=1859|1=1859}} ({{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 6), established. * {{ill|1900|en|1900|WD=Q2034|lt=1900|1=1900}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 33), upon a petition by the residents, the Ukemochi from the neglected {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} was enshrined alongside. * {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 8), driven by the sentiments of natives from Shiga Prefecture, the shrine building was reconstructed and the two deities known as Nakuin Nison ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}) from {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431089}} were additionally enshrined. * {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taishō 12), it was elevated to a village shrine and designated as a {{ill|Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|ja|神饌幣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* {{Myōken Faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinjinja|qq=}} [[ja:水天宮 (小樽市)]] {{translated page|ja|水天宮 (小樽市)|version=98159364|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q24316227}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:Designated Historic Buildings and structures in Otaru]] [[Category:Former_Gō-sha_in_Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Otaru]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in
